summary: First backend testing pass for FreshTrack Expiry & Inventory System. Wrote pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (37 tests) covering auth + RBAC (admin/manager/worker), products CRUD, status computation for general AND dairy category thresholds, duplicate detection (409 + duplicate alert), date validation, list filters (q/status_filter/category), dashboard summary, alerts (list/read/read-all/scan), thresholds (GET/PUT + invalid-range), reports summary + xlsx export (with PK header validation), and OCR endpoint with a generated JPEG product label (base64 form field). All 37 tests PASSED.

minor.issue: Default role for body.role=='admin' silently falls back to 'worker' (per spec) — but no explicit response field tells the client the role was overridden. Frontend may show confusing UX.

FRONTEND_URL='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec (browsers will reject credentialed requests). Works for the testing here because frontend uses Authorization header (Bearer token in localStorage), but should be tightened for production.
status_filter is applied AFTER limit=200, so a status-filtered list may return fewer than expected items when inventory grows large. Consider filtering in DB query or computing status in aggregation.
Both iterate every product and call enrich_product (which queries db.thresholds per product). Will not scale; cache thresholds per request.

action_items: Optional: cache thresholds within a single dashboard/report request to avoid N+1 lookups.
Optional: tighten CORS — replace allow_origins=['*'] with the actual REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL host when allow_credentials=True.
Optional: include a flag in /api/auth/register response when role was silently coerced to 'worker'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~860 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, products, alerts, ocr, reports) before it grows further.
enrich_product issues a per-product threshold query (N+1). Use an in-memory dict for one request.
JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_PASSWORD live in /app/backend/.env as plaintext — fine for dev but flag as standard ops note.
OCR confidence and needs_review logic correctly triggers an 'ocr_review' alert when needs_review is True.
Duplicate index on (product_name, batch_number) is unique — defense in depth alongside the explicit check. Good.
Date parser handles many formats including month-only and year-only (defaults to end-of-month / year-end) — robust.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/_label.b64 (new, generated JPEG label base64 for OCR test)
test_credentials: Admin admin@inventory.com / Admin@12345 (seeded). Test workers/managers created per run with TEST_* email prefix and password Pa$$word123.
seed_data_creation: Per-test-run unique worker and manager users (TEST_worker_<ts>@inventory.com, TEST_manager_<ts>@inventory.com). Test products prefixed 'TEST_Product_<ts>' are cleaned up at end of suite. Threshold 'TEST_cat_<ts>' is created but left behind (does not interfere with app).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. OCR uses Gemini gemini-3-flash-preview via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY and works with the generated label JPEG. /app/backend/tests/_label.b64 is a pre-generated base64 of a synthetic dairy-milk label and is reused across runs. Tests are idempotent — they create unique-per-run emails/products. For frontend testing, all backend contracts above are confirmed; the LandingPage / LoginPage / DashboardLayout etc. can be exercised against admin@inventory.com / Admin@12345 directly.
rca of the issue: No issues found — all flows behave as specified.
